title: A Relay-Depth Exponent Ladder Closing the Bettencourt 22-Indicator Urban-Scaling Panel
description: Working paper proposing an integer relay-depth exponent ladder beta_pm(H) = 1 pm 1/[H ln(2H+1)] that closes the canonical Bettencourt et al. (PNAS 2007) 22-indicator urban scaling panel at the 95% CI with zero fitted exponents. A zero-fitted-exponent complementary closure with integer, continuous, and hybrid assignments: 17/22 at single-integer H, 21/22 with continuous H_eff, 22/22 under a hybrid H in {2,3} cluster-mixture protocol (MAE 0.031, RMSE 0.045). The framework is pre-registered as falsifiable: a coverage drop to 21/22 or below at any audited release retires the closure. The 2007 panel is treated as the foundational empirical reference throughout; this manuscript is offered as a complementary refinement of that framework rather than a replacement. Release v1.3 (manuscript V22.6, 2026-05-16): substantive polish wave following the original author's first reply. The two reviewer questions raised in that reply (1) what is the mechanism and is it believable, and (2) what other predictions does the framework make, are now addressed directly in the manuscript body. Mechanism: section 1 Introduction and section 2.2 establish that the predicted exponent epsilon(H) = 1/[H ln(2H+1)] arises from a combinatorial-geometric construction (a signed alphabet A_H of size 2H+1 at each of H coordination rungs; the code volume (2H+1)^H is fixed by minimality), explicitly not from information-theoretic axioms. Predictions: section 3 Discussion adds four falsifiable orthogonal predictions: cross-time-beta ladder, size-dependent variance, out-of-sample blind exponents, and numerical reconciliation with canonical mean-field exponents. Bettencourt 2013 (Science 340, 1438) derivation flowchart, Bettencourt 2020 (Science Advances eaat8812) stochastic-growth framework, and Bettencourt 2021 (MIT Press, Introduction to Urban Science) are now cited as the foundational urban-scaling framework that the present ladder is a complementary refinement of. SI Appendix S-PanelOrigins now includes a full 22-row beta-independent assignment provenance table (indicator, network type, substrate constraint, branch, beta_obs, agreement rationale; 20/22 Match + 2 Qualified hybrid). Bibliography handle bumped V29 -> V30 to inject the Bettencourt 2013/2020/2021 entries. Central claim (22/22 hybrid closure, MAE 0.031, RMSE 0.045), all figures, scripts, simulation outputs, and the title 'Closing the Bettencourt 22-Indicator Urban-Scaling Panel' are unchanged from v1.2. The v1.0 (manuscript V206) and v1.1 (manuscript V207) drafts have been requested for deletion as they have been fully superseded by v1.2 (editorial cleanup) and v1.3 (Bettencourt-reply-driven substance polish).
